preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to an alveolar trill is a process of fortition or gemination, typically occurring to increase the perceptual salience of a rhotic or as a result of phonemic length contrasts.

examples.note: In many dialects, a tap can be realized as a trill for emphasis or in specific phonological contexts (e.g., word-initial position where historical taps were strengthened).
Historical shift where Latin geminate rhotics were reanalyzed as a distinct trill phoneme contrasted with the single tap.

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
